proven.claim: Proven: at line 321, args contains zero non-flag positional arguments
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Proven: at line 321, args contains zero non-flag positional arguments
; Transition: target = args.find((a) => !a.startsWith("-"))
; The path condition !target encodes: find() returned undefined
; i.e., every element starts with "-" OR the array is empty
(declare-const num_args Int)
(declare-const num_non_flags Int)
; args is a well-formed string array
(assert (>= num_args 0))
(assert (>= num_non_flags 0))
(assert (<= num_non_flags num_args))
; Path condition: !target → find() returned undefined → num_non_flags = 0
(assert (= num_non_flags 0))
; NEGATION: can a non-flag element exist at this point?
(assert (> num_non_flags 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Interpretation: proven — at line 321 no positional argument was present in args

proven.claim: Proven: at line 321, args length is either 0 OR all elements start with "-"
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; Proven: at line 321, args length is either 0 OR all elements start with "-"
; These are the only two ways args.find((a) => !a.startsWith("-")) returns undefined
(declare-const num_args Int)
(declare-const num_flags Int)
(declare-const num_non_flags Int)
; Transition: args.find partitions args into flags (start with "-") and non-flags
(assert (>= num_args 0))
(assert (>= num_flags 0))
(assert (>= num_non_flags 0))
(assert (= num_args (+ num_flags num_non_flags)))
; Path condition: !target → num_non_flags = 0
(assert (= num_non_flags 0))
; Either args is empty or all elements are flags — which case?
; Negation: can num_args > 0 AND num_non_flags > 0 simultaneously?
(assert (> num_args 0))
(assert (> num_non_flags 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; Interpretation: proven — if args is non-empty, every element must start with "-"

violations.claim: Reachable: line 321 triggered by boundary input — empty args
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; Reachable: line 321 triggered by boundary input — empty args
; User invokes: neurallog explain   (no file argument)
; process.argv.slice(2) = ["explain"]
; rest = ["explain"].slice(1) = []
; runExplain([]) → args.find(...) on empty array → undefined → !target → line 321
(declare-const argv_len Int)
(declare-const rest_len Int)
(declare-const num_non_flags Int)
; Transition: argv_len counts user args after "node neurallog"
; Typing only the subcommand name → argv_len = 1
(assert (= argv_len 1))
; Transition: rest = args.slice(1) strips the "explain" command token
(assert (= rest_len (- argv_len 1)))
; Transition: args.find() on an array of length rest_len
; num_non_flags is bounded by rest_len
(assert (>= num_non_flags 0))
(assert (<= num_non_flags rest_len))
; Line 321 condition: num_non_flags = 0 (find returned undefined)
(assert (= num_non_flags 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: argv_len=1, rest_len=0, num_non_flags=0
; Interpretation: reachable — 'neurallog explain' with no file triggers the usage error

violations.claim: Reachable: line 321 triggered by all-flag args (no positional argument)
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; Reachable: line 321 triggered by all-flag args (no positional argument)
; User invokes: neurallog explain --verbose  (flag only, no file:line)
; rest = ["--verbose"] — every element starts with "-"
; args.find((a) => !a.startsWith("-")) tests each element, all fail → returns undefined
(declare-const rest_len Int)
(declare-const num_flags Int)
(declare-const num_non_flags Int)
; Transition: caller provides at least one arg to runExplain
(assert (> rest_len 0))
; Transition: every element in rest starts with "-" (caller passes only flags)
(assert (= num_flags rest_len))
; Transition: num_non_flags = rest_len - num_flags
(assert (= num_non_flags (- rest_len num_flags)))
; Line 321 condition: num_non_flags = 0 (all args are flags, find returns undefined)
(assert (= num_non_flags 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: rest_len=1, num_flags=1, num_non_flags=0
; Interpretation: reachable — 'neurallog explain --verbose' with no file:line
;   triggers the usage error even though args is non-empty
